title: MIRL Provenance: an object file for dispersal and restitution
description: A no-build, local-first object-file and restitution-dossier tool for a single contested or dispersed object, or a body of them. Each object file holds an identity (titles in any script, type, materials, origin and community, identifiers), a dated chain of custody, sightings in the record, competing claims for return, a current holder and location with consent-aware publication, evidence hashed with sha-256 and held to chosen consent states, a register of sources whose identities never publish, CARE notes and Traditional Knowledge / Biocultural Labels (Local Contexts), an atlas of consented locations, a chronology, CSV import, serverless merge, fixity checks, passphrase encryption of the working file, and self-contained restitution-dossier, sought-notice, and public-file exports. Arabic and right-to-left ready. The nearest sibling of MIRL Lacuna.

creators.name: O'Brien, Jeff
creators.affiliation: Material / Image Research Lab, Department of History of Art & Architecture, University of California, Santa Barbara
